model: My headset isn't listed (generic VR profile)

connections.name: Tethered (DisplayPort / HDMI)
requirements: DisplayPort or HDMI cable direct to GPU

connections.name: USB cable with encoded video (Link-style)
requirements: USB 3.0+ cable rated for the headset's bitrate

connections.name: Wi-Fi wireless streaming
requirements: Vendor or third-party streaming app (VD / ALVR / etc.)

tracking.controllers: Varies — whatever ships with your headset

knownIssues.title: No headset-specific profile loaded — using generic VR defaults
knownIssues.description: You selected 'My headset isn't listed' so the suite is applying only general VR best-practice rules. Headset-specific issues (panel type, driver quirks, optimal supersampling, runtime-specific tweaks) cannot be surfaced without a matching profile.
knownIssues.workaround: If your headset is a popular model and you'd like a dedicated profile, open a Bug Report from Settings → Help & Feedback and tell the developer which headset you have. Profiles are added in roughly 80 lines of JSON per device and typically ship in the next update.

optimizationTips.tip: Start at 100% supersampling and adjust based on GPU headroom
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: Without a headset-specific profile, default to SteamVR render resolution 100%. If your GPU has headroom at 90 FPS, push up to 120–140%. If you're dropping frames, pull back to 80–90%.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: Use SteamVR's frame timing graph (dashboard → Settings → Developer → Frame Timing) to gate changes. Target sustained 90 FPS with GPU utilization around 80-85% for smooth VR with reprojection as a safety net.

optimizationTips.tip: Enable motion smoothing / ASW as a reprojection safety net
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: Most modern VR headsets support a form of reprojection that fills in frames when your PC can't keep up. Keep it enabled by default.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: SteamVR Motion Smoothing (for Valve/HTC/WMR) or Oculus ASW (for Meta) will halve your output frame rate and synthesize in-between frames from motion vectors when native render misses the frame budget. Visible ghosting is the failure mode but strongly preferable to hard stutter.

optimizationTips.tip: For wireless streaming, use Wi-Fi 6 5GHz minimum (Wi-Fi 6E 6GHz preferred)
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: Wireless VR needs ~100-200 Mbps sustained over Wi-Fi. 2.4 GHz is too slow and congested. Use a 5GHz or 6GHz-only SSID dedicated to your headset.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: Sustained 150 Mbps AV1 (Wi-Fi 6E) or 200 Mbps H.265 (Wi-Fi 6) is the common floor for acceptable wireless VR quality. Connect your streaming PC via Ethernet — running both ends on Wi-Fi doubles your jitter surface.
